reasons: Atlas's Fatal Links pulls multiple enemies into one spot, letting Nana land all of Molina Blitz on the clumped group for maximum AoE damage and the stun.
Locked-down enemies cannot escape Molina Blitz's casting delay, turning a hard-to-land skill into a guaranteed teamfight wipe.

proof.summary: Atlas grabs and slams multiple enemies into one location with Fatal Links; the grouped, immobilized targets are the ideal canvas for Nana's Molina Blitz, which repeatedly strikes an area and stuns anyone hit, converting Atlas's engage into a full AoE burst.
worksBestWhen: Enemies are clumped in the teamfight before Atlas commits.
Nana is positioned in range to drop Molina Blitz immediately after the slam.
Team has follow-up damage to finish the stunned group.
failureCases: Enemy uses Purify or Diggie ult to negate the Fatal Links pull.
Atlas engages alone and dies before Nana can follow.
Enemies are spread out so only one is caught.

reasons: Tigreal's Implosion ultimate sucks enemies into the center and stuns them, bunching them perfectly for Nana's Molina Blitz AoE.
The combined hard CC window gives Nana time to land her full slow-and-stun combo on multiple targets.

proof.summary: Tigreal's Implosion pulls enemies together and stuns them; Nana immediately layers Molina Blitz over the stunned cluster, chaining CC so the group is locked from Tigreal's stun straight into Molina's slow and continuous-hit stun.
worksBestWhen: Tigreal lands his ult on two or more enemies.
Nana stays backline-safe but in skill range.
Frontline can peel for Nana after she commits her combo.
failureCases: Tigreal's ult is dodged or flickered away from.
Nana is caught and bursted before casting.

reasons: Franco can Iron Hook a target Nana has slowed or transformed, then suppress them with Bloody Hunt so they have zero escape.
Nana's Molina Smooch slow makes Franco's notoriously hard-to-land hook far more reliable.

proof.summary: Nana's Molina Smooch slows and transforms a target, dramatically improving Franco's chance to land Iron Hook; he then drags the target into the team and locks it with Bloody Hunt suppression, creating an inescapable pick-off chain on the enemy carry.
worksBestWhen: Nana lands the slow or transform first to set up the hook.
Team can collapse on the hooked target.
failureCases: Target uses Purify to escape Nana's slow or transform before the hook lands (Bloody Hunt suppression itself cannot be purified).
Hook is body-blocked by a tank.
Franco hooks a tank instead of the priority carry.

reasons: Saber's Triple Sweep lifts and locks a single target; opening with Nana's transform or slow guarantees he reaches and bursts the trapped carry.
The transformed, helpless target cannot react during Saber's dive, securing a clean pick-off.

proof.summary: Nana transforms a priority target into a harmless cat or slows it with Molina Smooch, removing its ability to fight back; Saber then dives with Charge into Triple Sweep to knock the carry airborne and delete it during the lift before it can escape or retaliate.
worksBestWhen: Nana lands transform or slow before Saber commits.
Saber has flank position to reach the backline.
failureCases: Saber is caught out before Nana sets up.

reasons: Gusion's dagger-and-dash burst lands cleanly on a target slowed or transformed by Nana, and Molina Smooch's magic defense reduction amplifies his magic burst.
The combined CC plus magic-resist reduction lets Gusion delete squishies he might otherwise miss.

proof.summary: Nana's Molina Smooch slows and reduces the target's magic defense, making it both easier for Gusion to land his Sword Spike and Shadowblade Slaughter combo and far more lethal, since the magic-resist reduction stacks with his already high magic burst to delete the carry.
worksBestWhen: Nana lands Molina Smooch on the priority target first.
Gusion has the cooldowns ready to dive immediately.
Target is a squishy mage or marksman.
failureCases: Target purifies or flickers out of the slow.
Gusion mistimes daggers and the slow expires.
Enemy frontline shields or peels the target.
